Slim Aarons: La Dolce Vita, the coffee-table classic of golden-age Italy → https://amzn.to/3S4SWGh

comments.author: @barbarasmith6229
comments.text: I wish the people (?) who put this together learned that tomatoes don’t arrive in Europe until approximately the mid-1500’s and are native to central and South America. Please. Ancient neapolitans don’t have tomatoes on their pizza.  Duh. That factual error calls into question everything said here. Shame on you.

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: You're right on the timeline — appreciate you catching that. We'll take a closer look at where that gets used in the narration and correct it.

replies.author: @marzie82
replies.text: It never said the ancient neapolitans eat pizza. They said it was invented about the 17-18 hundreds by the poor, well after tomatoes came to Europe. Learn to listen rather than criticise, DUH!

comments.author: @EpicAdventureWorld
comments.text: Which place would you choose: Naples, Iceland, or Japan if you had to live near an active volcano?

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: Naples, easily — Vesuvius has the better view to go with the risk.

replies.author: @barbarasmith6229
replies.text: Iceland’s volcanoes are shield volcanoes, not stratovolcanoes.   They’re not as explosive as what’s in Japan or Vesuvius and Campi Fleigri.

comments.author: @MrParondo
comments.text: 😂 try driving a car in Napoli ! 😂 avanti avanti !

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: Naples traffic is its own civilizational achievement. Thank you for watching. 🌿

comments.author: @maurachapman4179
comments.text: Over the past couple weeks this channel has become my bedtime stories.

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: That's exactly the kind of companion we set out to be. More journeys ahead. 🌿

comments.author: @ChanelExploreTravel4K
comments.text: What strikes me most about Naples is the contrast. Few cities have lived under the shadow of an active volcano for thousands of years, yet life here feels incredibly vibrant. History is everywhere, but the city never feels trapped in the past. It feels like a place that has learned to live with both beauty and uncertainty.
